description:
## Background

Beadsmith currently runs every `bw` command in inherited process cwd and reports that cwd as the Current Workspace. That is incompatible with runtime switching: cwd is global to the process, and a command started during or after a switch could observe the wrong repository. Beadwork can also validate a selected subdirectory by walking up to a repository root, so command success alone is not a Workspace identity.

## What to build

Create the path-scoped Workspace command and validation boundary. Backend callers can validate a candidate path into exactly one Beadwork Git-root Workspace and load All Issues, Ready, and Blocked using that root explicitly. The boundary is testable without a real Beadwork installation and has a typed, user-displayable distinction between validation failures and later issue-loading failures.

## Technical Approach

- Extend the existing command-runner seam so a caller supplies an explicit working directory for every subprocess. Keep command construction and output interpretation inside the backend boundary.
- Add a focused repository-location operation. It must validate the candidate with Beadwork configuration inspection and independently discover the Git repository root; configuration output is never parsed as an identity.
- Normalize a valid selected subdirectory to the discovered root before any catalog or loader caller can observe it. Reject non-Git, uninitialized, unreadable, or otherwise invalid candidates with typed validation outcomes.
- Use the same injected runner/fake strategy for `bw` and repository-root discovery. If the required local Git command is unavailable, surface a clear validation/execution error rather than falling back to process cwd.
- Convert the new Issue Explorer adapter path to require an explicit root for All, Ready, and Blocked. Leave the old startup-cwd caller isolated only until the initial-selection bead migrates it.

## Considerations

- A Workspace is the initialized repository root, not a descendant directory and not arbitrary Beadwork configuration output.
- This slice performs read-only validation and view loading; it must not mutate catalog or Current Workspace state.
- Command calls may be slow or fail. The later switch service owns user cancellation, generation, persistence, and UI feedback; this boundary must return enough typed context for it to do so.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The runner and adapter can run every Beadwork Issue Explorer command against a caller-supplied root without consulting process cwd.
- [ ] Validation returns the Git root for a valid selected subdirectory and never lets that subdirectory become a separate Workspace identity.
- [ ] Non-Git, uninitialized Beadwork, unreadable, missing-Git, and command-execution failures are distinguishable at the validation boundary.
- [ ] New production loader code has no `env::current_dir` or runtime cwd mutation path.
- [ ] Existing behavior remains buildable through the temporary compatibility caller until first-time selection migrates it.

## Test Plan

- Fake-runner unit tests for explicit cwd on All, Ready, and Blocked commands.
- Unit tests for root normalization, invalid directories, uninitialized repositories, missing command binaries, and root-discovery failures.
- Adapter contract tests proving view results preserve existing Beadwork semantics after cwd becomes explicit.
- Run Rust tests and generated typed-RPC contract checks relevant to the new boundary.
title: Establish explicit Workspace command scope and validation
